title: Oh, Wert Thou In The Cauld Blast.
content:
Tune - "Lass o' Livistone."

I.

        Oh, wert thou in the cauld blast,
            On yonder lea, on yonder lea,
        My plaidie to the angry airt,
            I'd shelter thee, I'd shelter thee:
        Or did misfortune's bitter storms
            Around thee blaw, around thee blaw,
        Thy bield should be my bosom,
            To share it a', to share it a'.

II.

        Or were I in the wildest waste,
            Sae black and bare, sae black and bare,
        The desert were a paradise,
            If thou wert there, if thou wert there:
        Or were I monarch o' the globe,
            Wi' thee to reign, wi' thee to reign,
        The brightest jewel in my crown
            Wad be my queen, wad be my queen.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Oh, Wert Thou In The Cauld Blast.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
